note_data.title: Newton's laws of motion
keyPoints: **Newton's First Law**: A body remains at rest or moves with constant velocity unless acted upon by a resultant force
**Newton's Second Law**: The rate of change of momentum is proportional to the resultant force acting on the body, F = ma when mass is constant
**Newton's Third Law**: If body A exerts a force on body B, then body B exerts an equal and opposite force on body A
For **connected particles** via light inextensible string, both particles have the same acceleration magnitude
In **pulley systems**, tension is the same throughout a light inextensible string passing over a smooth pulley
When objects are in a **lift**, apparent weight changes: add 'ma' to actual weight if accelerating upwards, subtract 'ma' if accelerating downwards
For objects on **smooth surfaces**, normal reaction equals weight component perpendicular to surface
**Resultant force** in direction of motion equals mass × acceleration in that direction
For **equilibrium**, resolve forces in two perpendicular directions and set each sum equal to zero

definitions.definition: A string that has negligible mass and does not stretch under tension
A pulley that rotates freely without friction and does not affect the tension in the string
The single force that has the same effect as all the individual forces acting on a body
The contact force acting perpendicular to a surface that prevents objects passing through the surface

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Work through the calculation step-by-step to reach the given result
Calculate the value using appropriate equations of motion or force equations

modelAnswer.question: Two particles A and B of masses 3 kg and 2 kg respectively are connected by a light inextensible string passing over a smooth pulley. Particle A hangs vertically and B rests on a smooth horizontal table. Find the acceleration of the system and the tension in the string.
modelAnswer.answer:
Let acceleration be a m/s² and tension be T N.
For particle A (taking downward as positive): 3g - T = 3a
For particle B (taking rightward as positive): T = 2a
Substituting: 3g - 2a = 3a
30 - 2a = 3a
30 = 5a
a = 6 m/s²
T = 2 × 6 = 12 N
markBreakdown: Mark 2: Apply F = ma to particle A correctly
Mark 3: Apply F = ma to particle B correctly

commonMistakes: Never ignore the direction of acceleration - always define positive direction clearly for each particle
Students often forget that tension acts upward on the hanging mass and horizontally on the mass on the table
Never assume tension equals weight of lighter particle - this is only true when acceleration is zero
Students often confuse apparent weight with actual weight in lift problems
Never use different accelerations for connected particles - they must have the same magnitude
examinerTips: This topic appears almost every year in Paper 4 Mechanics
Always draw a clear force diagram for each particle separately
Define your positive direction before writing equations of motion
In lift problems, always consider whether the lift is accelerating up or down
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Draw separate force diagrams for each particle showing weight, tension, normal reactions and any other forces. Use arrows to indicate positive direction of motion for each particle.
